title: pinus balfouriana subsp. aristata
intro: Pinus balfouriana subsp. aristata, also known as the Bristlecone Pine, is a slow-growing evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Mexico. It is valued for its unique, twisted trunks and branches that hold medicinal properties, particularly in the resin and needles, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Pinus balfouriana subsp. aristata are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, offering health benefits such as relieving coughs, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations include essential oils, infusions, and salves made from the resin and needles, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce pain, and support immune function.
